# A04G Layout (S26)

## ESE 5160: IoT Edge Computing Deadline: Feb 23, 202611:59 PM EST

A **group** submission on Gradescope.

**Late-day requests:** [https://forms.gle/7hZVrXLZ51XiCExm8](https://forms.gle/7hZVrXLZ51XiCExm8) 

Course Webpage: [https://ese5160.seas.upenn.edu/](https://ese5160.seas.upenn.edu/)	

Remember to read the [ESE5160 S26 Assignment README](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1DQJxfhTMBN1aE2uasZiKpqR0JOT77pXvb6tUPF7iKF8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.c5nyj2pvdk6p) before starting\!

**All the required questions are highlighted in green.**

**Important notes are highlighted in yellow.**

# Learning Outcomes

* Understand how current and temperature are related on printed circuit boards.  
* Learn what best practices are used on the Altium starter project, such as how to protect signal lines from ESD and noise.  
* Understand the importance of circuit board layout \- how it affects all aspects of the PCB’s functionality and determines its success.

# Errata

This section includes commonly asked questions about the assignment. It will be updated continuously throughout the assignment’s lifecycle, so please check it regularly.

(Currently nothing)

# 

# 0\. Order Your Most Risky Component

For your project, you will need several breakout boards to prototype with the **BRD2708A development board** before receiving your custom PCBs. To ensure smooth progress, you must **order 1x development/breakout boards for your most critical component now**. Failure to do so in time may prevent you from completing **Assignment A08**.

Note: If your dev board exceeds $35, ask your account manager for approval.

**Submission:** Order your Most Risky Component using this form [https://forms.gle/t21AdA35igJYsziRA](https://forms.gle/t21AdA35igJYsziRA) 

# 1\. PCB Hot Topics

1. If you wanted to run a trace with 1A of current on the top or bottom signal layer, how thick should it be on our circuit board? 10\*C rise is acceptable. You should use the [Layer Stack Manager](https://www.altium.com/documentation/altium-designer/defining-layer-stack) to review critical stackup elements. **Show how you got to this answer.**  
   1. Note: Make sure to use [polygon pours](https://www.altium.com/documentation/altium-designer/pcb-signal-layer-polygons) in your team’s PCB design \- they’re perfect for handling heavy current loads (think: all of your power circuitry\!)  
2. What will happen if a PCB trace is thinner than needed for the current it carries?  
3. Describe the purpose of thermal reliefs in the context of PCB design.  
4. In the Altium starter project schematics **Top Level** and **Memory**, identify the design elements that protect against electrostatic discharge (ESD) and noise on the power and signal lines.  
5. Review the **BQ24075** datasheet. What is the ESD rating?  
6. Review TI’s app note on [Semiconductor and IC Package Thermal Metrics](https://www.ti.com/lit/an/spra953c/spra953c.pdf?ts=1695811141104).  
   1. What is RθJA and how does it affect our PCB layout?  
   2. What factors affect RθJA?

(1.1) On Gradescope, submit your answers to these questions.

# 2\. PCB Layout

Now that your schematics are complete, you will perform your complete PCB layout. Ensure you review the lectures and the [PCB Design Review Checklist ESE5160 S26](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ipyIuc_Ly_mjgZjhHJGv7Ep6g4OOlyAYM76pn7uFIx8/edit?tab=t.0) at least twice: once before starting and once before submitting.

Don’t forget \- the professor has created short Altium videos for you:  [altium videos upenn ese](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/10oZzWaeaywpYi4FAVDh5ER4aY_bC2vUl?usp=sharing)

**PCBA Specifications**

| The PCB must be smaller than or equal to 3600 mm2 (such as 60mm x 60mm) If you need more space, please contact the teaching team; we can usually work with you within reason. You’re encouraged to have fun with different PCB shapes and sizes. If you’re feeling up to it, use SolidWorks to do your board shape using the [MCAD CoDesigner tool](https://www.altium.com/documentation/altium-codesigner/preparing-solidworks-for-codesign). Get a SolidWorks license [here](https://cets.seas.upenn.edu/answers/solidworks.html). Inspiration from previous years: ![][image1]![][image2] |
| ----- |
| **Single-sided surface mount assembly** This means that when the PCBAs are made, the manufacturer will solder all surface mount components on the top side of the PCB. They will **not** solder any through-hole components or any components on the bottom of the PCB. **Note:** you can hand assemble components on the bottom if they allow easy rework.Through-hole components and larger surface mount components are good for this. |
| Review the lecture slides on planning your PCB and doing the layout\! Don’t forget to reference [altium videos upenn ese](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/10oZzWaeaywpYi4FAVDh5ER4aY_bC2vUl) for tutorials as well. |
| Be sure to implement all the comments from A03G before you start routing. |
| **Before starting** & **before your final submission**, read the [PCB Design Review Checklist ESE5160 S26](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ipyIuc_Ly_mjgZjhHJGv7Ep6g4OOlyAYM76pn7uFIx8/edit?tab=t.0) in its entirety. (So important, we added this text twice\!) |
| For this assignment, make sure you can pass all Tier 1 requirements in [PCB Design Review Checklist ESE5160 S26](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ipyIuc_Ly_mjgZjhHJGv7Ep6g4OOlyAYM76pn7uFIx8/edit?tab=t.0) |
| Add a custom Team Logo to your PCBA  (see below). |

# Team Logo

Trick out your PCB with your own custom logo\! The picture of the logo can be anything you want to appear on the PCB (as long as it is appropriate for the classroom).

Submit a monochrome picture of your desired logo and place the logo on your PCB. It can be whatever you want\! An option you can use is to use icons from the following website, which offers free icons to use: [https://www.flaticon.com/](https://www.flaticon.com/)

Here are some examples of logos on a board that used a combination of free icons from this website. Note that some teams used both the silkscreen & exposed copper layers to make a unique design.

| ![][image3] | ![][image4] | ![][image5] |
| :---: | :---: | :---: |
| ![][image6] | ![][image7] | ![][image8] |

**(2.1)** On Gradescope, add a link to your A365 project after committing all your work for the PCB layout.  
Ensure you’ve added your logo to your PCB, making sure it’s on a visible layer  
(the top or bottom silkscreen is typical, but you can do fancy things with your copper layering as well).

# Rubric

See the Gradescope assignment for the rubric details. For all questions, 0 points may be awarded if the submission is non-existent, very poorly done, or doesn’t compile (for firmware assignments).

This is a pass/fail assignment.
